fd.name: Observation (children — standard of care)
fd.note: No pain, no functional impairment — monitor until skeletal maturity.

fd.name: Bisphosphonates (pamidronate, zoledronate)
fd.note: Standard for bone pain. IV cycles every 3-6 months.

fd.name: Denosumab
fd.note: For rapidly progressive lesions — RANKL blockade. Strict calcium and 25-OH-D monitoring required.

fd.name: Burosumab
fd.note: Only when FGF23-dependent hypophosphataemia is documented. New — consensus since 2024.

fd.name: Surgery
fd.note: Reserved for clinical necessity — optic nerve compression, severe pain unresponsive to medical therapy, severe deformity. Generally deferred until skeletal maturity because of high recurrence in growing bone, but exceptions exist when function or vision is at risk.

fd.name: MEK inhibitors
fd.note: Preliminary mouse-model data. No clinical evidence yet.

mas.name: Endocrine treatment (per endocrinopathy)
mas.note: Letrozole for precocious puberty in girls; somatostatin analogues for GH excess; thyroid-sparing strategies for hyperthyroidism.

mas.name: Observation (calcium/phosphate/endocrine monitoring)
mas.note: Annual endocrine and metabolic panel; DXA every 1–2 years; ophthalmology and audiology if craniofacial FD present.

mas.name: Bisphosphonates (pamidronate, zoledronate)
mas.note: First-line for FD-related bone pain and progressive lesions in MAS; IV pamidronate or zoledronate every 3–6 months. Evidence from FD/MAS cohort studies.

mas.name: Denosumab
mas.note: RANKL blockade for rapidly progressive FD lesions in MAS; monitor calcium and 25-OH-D closely. Risk of rebound hypercalcaemia on discontinuation.

noonan.name: Recombinant growth hormone (rGH)
noonan.note: FDA-approved for Noonan-related short stature. Higher dose than in GH deficiency.

noonan.name: Cardiology monitoring
noonan.note: ECHO every 1-2 years; more frequent with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

noonan.name: MEK inhibitors (trametinib, selumetinib)
noonan.note: Off-label in severe HCM. Phase 2 trials in progress.
